sections.heading: Where do I find a breakdown of costs?
sections.body:
A breakdown of costs for all MongoDB Cloud product usage can be found
on your monthly cloud invoices.

To view a breakdown of costs:

sections.heading: How can I better allocate my costs?
sections.body:
In addition to the default breakdown of costs that your
invoice provides, you can configure your own
[custom tags](configure-resource-tags)
for a deployment to categorize costs. To learn more,
see [Resource Tags on Invoices](resource-tags-on-invoices).

> **Important:** Resource tags that appear on invoice CSV (Comma-Separated Values) files and those returned
> from the Atlas Admin API reflect your configuration of
> resource tags at the moment the CSV is generated or the API is invoked.
>
> Please be aware that if you reconfigure your resource tags within a
> billing cycle, your invoice reflects only the latest configuration of tags.

sections.heading: Why does my invoice include usage from last month?
sections.body:
Cloud usage at the very end of a billing period may appear
as a line item on your invoice for the next billing period instead. For
example, usage on October 31st may be processed in November.

Atlas data transfer, private endpoint, and backup costs depend on your
Cloud Service Provider. Due to reporting lag from Cloud Service
Providers, usage of these services may be processed in the following
days.

sections.heading: How can I reduce my data transfer costs?
sections.body:
There are a number of ways to optimize data transference depending on
the configuration of your project. For a list of common options, see
[How to Reduce Data Transfer Costs](reducing-data-transfer-costs).

sections.heading: What happens if I do not pay my invoice?
sections.body:
If your organization fails to pay at least one invoice for over 30 days, we
suspend your MongoDB Atlas account and automatically stop your backups without
terminating them. You must pay any outstanding bills to resume service.

If your organization fails to pay an invoice balance for over 60 days, we
lock your MongoDB Atlas account and can terminate backups and Atlas deployments
connected to your account. You must pay all outstanding bills to resume service.

sections.heading: Why did my Atlas payment fail?
sections.body:
Payments will fail if there is an issue with your
**Payment Method**. To learn how to verify your
**Payment Method** and retry your payment, see
[Retry a Failed Payment](retry-failed-payment).

Customers in the European Economic Area (EEA) may experience payment
failures due to Strong Customer Authentication (SCA), a European
regulatory requirement. To learn how to authenticate a credit card and
comply with SCA (Strong Customer Authentication), see
[Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) Changes](sca-changes).

sections.heading: How can I pay for an Atlas subscription with a credit card?
sections.body:
To pay for an [elastic billing subscription](atlas-subscriptions) with a
credit card:

- Contact [MongoDB Sales](https://www.mongodb.com/company/contact?tck=docs) to purchase a
  subscription.

- Email a request to [ar@mongodb.com](mailto:ar@mongodb.com) to pay for your active
  subscription with a credit card. Please include your Organization ID
  in your request.

You can find your Organization ID in the Atlas UI, under your
organization's **General Settings**.

sections.heading: How can I pay for MongoDB Atlas without a credit card?
sections.body:
You can pay with [PayPal](https://www.paypal.com/us/home)
through the Atlas console.

For alternative payment methods, or to purchase an Atlas
subscription, please contact [MongoDB Sales](https://www.mongodb.com/company/contact?tck=docs).

sections.heading: What happens when I exhaust my Atlas credits and move to elastic billing?
sections.body:
When you exhaust your Atlas credits and you have an
[elastic billing subscription](atlas-subscriptions), you can
continue to use Atlas without interruption. You're billed for
usage beyond your credits with monthly invoices.

Your organization activity feed produces an event notifying you that
MongoDB will invoice your organization for your monthly usage. The
[payment method](payment-method) section of your organization's
billing overview reflects the change.

sections.heading: Has MongoDB received my invoice payment?
sections.body:
With a MongoDB subscription, you can check if MongoDB has
received a payment for an invoice in the Atlas console:

sections.heading: Step 1: Review your invoice.
sections.body:
- Click on the invoice for which you want to check your payment
  status.

- Scroll down to the **Payments** section of the
  invoice. The **Status** column lists your payment
  status.

  If MongoDB has received a payment, the payment status is
  `SUCCESSFUL`.

  To learn more, see the [Payments](payment-statuses)
  statuses.

sections.heading: Where can I find MongoDB's bank details?
sections.body:
With a MongoDB subscription, MongoDB’s bank details are listed
on the tax invoice emailed to your billing contact.

To learn more, see [Pay Subscription Charges](pay-subscription-charges).

sections.heading: How do I update my billing contact information?
sections.body:
To update your billing contact information, email a request to
[ar@mongodb.com](mailto:ar@mongodb.com). Please include your company's name, your Organization
ID, and your new billing contact information.

You can find your Organization ID in the Atlas UI, under your
organization's General Settings.
